summary: Partially decompiled and typed portions of src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_0A01.c, replacing several NOT_IMPLEMENTED/inline-ASM stubs with C for small accessors, boolean checks, pad scaling helpers, hurtbox extent calculation, and an update wrapper. The PR also tightened ftCo_0A01.h prototypes/static qualifiers, expanded Fighter fields around fp+1A88, introduced IntVec2 in dolphin mtx types, and adjusted related player/stage/header declarations. The PR body reports ftCo_0A01.c at 1.18% file mismatch remaining, with many functions already 0.00% and a handful still partially or fully unmatched.
what_changed: Replaced the asm body of ftCo_800A0C8C with a simple C wrapper calling ftCo_800B463C(arg0, 127).

Added a C implementation for ftCo_800A0DA4 that iterates fighter hurtboxes, calls lbColl_800083C4, computes horizontal/vertical extents relative to cur_pos and scale, mirrors values based on facing_dir, and writes the results through M2C_FIELD offsets from x1A88.

Added C implementations for small ftCo_0A01 helpers including ftCo_800A17E4, ftCo_800A1948, ftCo_800A198C, ftCo_800A1C44, ftCo_800A1CA8, ftCo_800A2040, ftCo_800A2BD4, ftCo_800A3200, ftCo_800A49B4, ftCo_800A59C0, and ftCo_800B3900, while some remained ASM/NOT_IMPLEMENTED.
Updated ftCo_0A01.h from broad UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS declarations and commented-out static markers toward concrete return types, argument names, bool/float/HSD_Pad returns, and real static qualifiers.

Expanded struct Fighter in src/melee/ft/types.h around fp+1A88: added HSD_Pad x1A88, byte fields x1A8C through x1A93, x1AA0-style unknown pointer slots, float x1ADC, and changed x2338 from Vec2 to IntVec2.
Added IntVec2 forward declaration and struct definition in dolphin mtx headers.
Changed Player_8003248C return type from s32 to enum_t in player.c/player.h and used that typed result from ftCo_800A2040.
Adjusted grCorneria_801E2D90 and grInishie1_801FCAAC prototypes to bool functions taking enum_t.
Removed duplicate/obsolete ftCo declarations from ft_0877.dox and ft_08A4.h and updated ftCo_800B463C to void ftCo_800B463C(UNK_T, int).
Moved some data/jump-table declarations under MUST_MATCH and changed WIP pragma handling from always_active to force_active near ftCo_0A01 data/prototypes.
smart_moves: Started with low-risk leaf functions and direct accessors: ftCo_800A198C simply returns fp->x1A88, while ftCo_800A0C8C reduces to a one-call wrapper with constant 127.
Used newly typed Fighter fields to turn bit/offset tests into readable conditions, for example fp->x2219_b1, fp->x2164, fp->x2168, fp->x2338.x, and fp->x221F_b3 in ftCo_800A1C44.
Preserved matching-sensitive code by keeping larger or harder functions as ASM while converting only selected functions to C.
Added struct/header types before relying on them in decompiled functions, notably HSD_Pad for fp+1A88 and IntVec2 for fp+2338.
Kept M2C_FIELD for offsets that were not yet fully typed, allowing partial progress on ftCo_800A0DA4 and ftCo_800A49B4 without overclaiming struct knowledge.
Updated prototypes and static qualifiers alongside implementation changes, reducing cross-header ambiguity and helping the compiler emit closer code.
decomp_lessons: When a function returns a packed or known field, create the field type in the owning struct and update the return type rather than leaving the function as UNK_RET.
Partial decompilation can safely mix C and ASM in the same file if matching-sensitive functions remain guarded by MUST_MATCH/WIP patterns.
Offset-heavy fighter code benefits from incremental struct recovery: adding a small typed window around fp+1A88 enabled several helper functions to become readable C.
Use integer vector types when assembly treats fields as integer words; this PR changed Fighter.x2338 from Vec2 to IntVec2 and introduced IntVec2 globally.
For tiny boolean helpers, reproducing exact control flow with explicit true/false returns can eliminate whole inline-ASM blocks.
Header cleanup is part of matching work: concrete prototypes, argument types, and static visibility affect codegen and prevent mismatched declarations across modules.
Retain M2C_FIELD for unresolved substructure offsets instead of prematurely naming unknown nested fields.
naming_conventions: Fighter offset-backed fields use x-offset names such as x1A88, x1A8C, x1A91, x1ADC, x2164, x2168, x2219_b1, x221F_b3, x221F_b4, and x2338.
Boolean bitfields in Fighter use suffixes such as _b1, _b3, and _b4 when individual bits are known.
Common fighter functions retain address-style ftCo_800Axxxx names, with typed prototypes added as knowledge improves.
Pointer arguments in prototypes are named fp for Fighter/ftCo_Fighter and gobj for Fighter_GObj.
New vector type IntVec2 follows existing dolphin mtx naming alongside IntVec3, S32Vec2, S32Vec3, S16Vec3, and S8Vec3.
Static-only internal functions are declared with actual static rather than comments like /* static */.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Converted small inline-ASM bodies to ordinary C where the assembly was a direct wrapper, field load, switch, comparison, or clamp.
Kept remaining unmatched functions as ASM with MUST_MATCH/WIP guards rather than forcing incomplete C.
Used M2C_FIELD casts for writes to unknown offsets from fp and x1A88 while still exposing surrounding high-level logic.
For square-root-like distance code in ftCo_800A49B4, used __frsqrte and Newton refinement-style arithmetic matching the assembly pattern.
Moved label/jump-table declarations under MUST_MATCH so label-only assembly artifacts are not exposed during non-matching C builds.
Used explicit constants such as 127, 127.0f, 255.0, 0.0078125f, 0.5, 1.0, and -1.0 to mirror scalar conversion/clamp code.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or review records were present in the PR slice, so no human review feedback can be inferred.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that fully matched the remaining ftCo_0A01 functions listed as 100.00% or partial in PR #912.
Search for follow-up struct Fighter work around x1A88 through x1AE0 to replace M2C_FIELD uses with named fields.
Check later changes to ftCo_800A0DA4 and ftCo_800A49B4 for better names for the x1A88-relative offsets.
Look for later confirmation of IntVec2 usage at Fighter.x2338 and whether other Vec2-to-IntVec2 corrections were made.
Search for later cleanup of ftCo_0A01.h static qualifiers and duplicated ftCo prototypes across ft_08A4.h and ft_0877.dox.
